functions.name: addPerlApi
functions.description: Adds a Perl API server resource (e.g., Mojolicious, Dancer2) to the application model. Passes the `daemon` subcommand so HTTP frameworks start a listener.
functions.remarks:
This method configures a Perl API script and passes the default API subcommand
(`daemon`) so frameworks such as Mojolicious start an HTTP server.
The working directory is set to `appDirectory` and all relative paths
(including `WithLocalLib` and cpanfile discovery) resolve against it.
functions.signature: addPerlApi(resourceName: string, appDirectory: string, scriptName: string): PerlAppResource

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the Perl script. Resolved relative to the AppHost project directory; becomes the resource's working directory and the anchor for all relative path resolution (script path, `WithLocalLib`, cpanfile discovery).

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: The API script path, relative to `appDirectory`. Do not include the `appDirectory` prefix ﻗ the script is resolved relative to `appDirectory` automatically.

functions.name: addPerlExecutable
functions.description: Adds a Perl executable (compiled binary or PAR-packed application) to the application model. The executable is run directly rather than through the `perl` interpreter.
functions.signature: addPerlExecutable(resourceName: string, appDirectory: string, executablePath: string): PerlAppResource

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the working directory for the application.

parameters.name: executablePath
parameters.description: The path to the executable, relative to `appDirectory`.

functions.name: addPerlModule
functions.description: Adds a Perl module to the application model. The module is executed using `perl -MModule::Name -e "Module::Name->run()"`.
functions.signature: addPerlModule(resourceName: string, appDirectory: string, moduleName: string): PerlAppResource

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the working directory for the application.

parameters.name: moduleName
parameters.description: The fully qualified Perl module name (e.g., `"MyApp::Main"`).

functions.name: addPerlScript
functions.description: Adds a Perl script resource (worker, CLI tool, background service) to the application model.
functions.remarks:
This method executes a Perl script directly using `perl -s scriptName`.
The working directory is set to `appDirectory` and all relative paths
(including `WithLocalLib` and cpanfile discovery) resolve against it.
functions.signature: addPerlScript(resourceName: string, appDirectory: string, scriptName: string): PerlAppResource

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the Perl script. Resolved relative to the AppHost project directory; becomes the resource's working directory and the anchor for all relative path resolution (script path, `WithLocalLib`, cpanfile discovery).

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: The path to the script relative to `appDirectory`. Do not include the `appDirectory` prefix ﻗ the script is resolved relative to `appDirectory` automatically.

functions.name: withCarton
functions.description: Configures the Perl application to use Carton as its package manager. Carton manages dependencies via `cpanfile` and a lock file (`cpanfile.snapshot`), enabling reproducible builds. Use `WithProjectDependencies``1` to run `carton install` at startup.
functions.remarks:
Carton only supports project-level dependency installation via `cpanfile`.
Calling `WithPackage``1` after `WithCarton()` will throw
because Carton does not support installing individual modules.
To install Carton, you may use "cpanm Carton".

functions.name: withCpanMinus
functions.description: Configures the Perl application to use cpanm (App::cpanminus) as its package manager instead of the default cpan. Call this before `WithPackage``1` to change how packages are installed.

functions.name: withLocalLib
functions.description: Configures the Perl application to use a local::lib directory for module isolation. Sets `PERL5LIB`, `PERL_LOCAL_LIB_ROOT`, `PERL_MM_OPT`, and `PERL_MB_OPT` environment variables so that modules are resolved from and installed into the local directory.
functions.remarks:
Sets the following environment variables:
-
-
-
-
These ensure modules are resolved from and installed into the local directory
without requiring system-level permissions.
If the active package manager is `cpan` (the default), it is automatically
switched to `cpanm` since `cpan` does not support the `--local-lib` flag.
While it is possible to use `cpan` with Local::Lib it is complicated to model in Aspire.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to the local::lib directory. Relative paths are resolved against the resource's working directory (`appDirectory`), not the AppHost project. Rooted paths (e.g., `/opt/perl-libs` or `C:\perl-libs`) are used as-is. Defaults to `"local"` (the Carton convention).

functions.name: withPackage
functions.description: Adds a Perl package (module) to be installed before the application starts. Uses the configured package manager: cpan by default, or cpanm if `WithCpanMinus``1` was called.
functions.remarks:
A child installer resource is created that runs before the main application starts.
If the module is already installed (verified via `perl -MModuleName -e 1`),
installation is skipped. The installer uses the active package manager ﻗ
`cpan` by default, or `cpanm` if `WithCpanMinus``1`
was called.
functions.signature: withPackage(packageName: string, force?: boolean, skipTest?: boolean): PerlAppResource

parameters.name: packageName
parameters.description: The name of the Perl module to install (e.g., "Mojolicious", "DBI").

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: If `true`, force installation even if the module is already installed or tests fail.

parameters.name: skipTest
parameters.description: If `true`, skip running tests during installation.

functions.name: withPerlbrew
functions.description: Configures the Perl application to use a specific perlbrew-managed Perl version.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The perlbrew version name. Accepts both `"5.38.0"` and `"perl-5.38.0"`.

parameters.name: perlbrewRoot
parameters.description: Optional explicit path to the perlbrew root directory. If `null`, resolves from the `PERLBREW_ROOT` environment variable, or defaults to `~/perl5/perlbrew`.

functions.name: withPerlbrewEnvironment
functions.description: Configures the Perl application to use a specific perlbrew-managed Perl version. This resolves the Perl executable from the perlbrew installation and updates the resource's command and environment variables so that all subsequent operations use the specified Perl version.
functions.remarks:
Perlbrew is Linux-only. On Windows, an `InvalidOperationException` is thrown
with a recommendation to use Berrybrew.
When combined with `WithLocalLib``1`, modules are installed into the
local directory rather than the perlbrew tree, keeping the perlbrew installation clean
and enabling per-project module isolation.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The perlbrew version name. Accepts both `"5.38.0"` and `"perl-5.38.0"`.

parameters.name: perlbrewRoot
parameters.description: Optional explicit path to the perlbrew root directory. If `null`, resolves from the `PERLBREW_ROOT` environment variable, or defaults to `~/perl5/perlbrew`.

functions.name: withPerlCertificateTrust
functions.description: Configures certificate trust for the Perl application by setting SSL/TLS environment variables that common Perl HTTP libraries respect. Sets `SSL_CERT_FILE` (IO::Socket::SSL / LWP), `PERL_LWP_SSL_CA_FILE` (LWP::UserAgent), and `MOJO_CA_FILE` (Mojolicious) to the certificate bundle path provided by Aspire.

functions.name: withProjectDependencies
functions.description: Configures project-level dependency installation for the Perl application. Runs the appropriate install command based on the active package manager: - - If the active package manager is `cpan` (the default), it is automatically switched to `cpanm` since `cpan` does not support `--installdeps`.
functions.remarks:
Expects a `cpanfile` in the resource's working directory (`appDirectory`).
If the cpanfile is in a different location, adjust `appDirectory` accordingly.
When using Carton with `cartonDeployment` set to `true`,
a `cpanfile.snapshot` must also be present.

parameters.name: cartonDeployment
parameters.description: If `true` and using Carton, adds `--deployment` for reproducible installs from `cpanfile.snapshot`. Ignored for other package managers.

capabilities.name: withCarton
capabilities.description: Configures the Perl application to use Carton as its package manager. Carton manages dependencies via `cpanfile` and a lock file (`cpanfile.snapshot`), enabling reproducible builds. Use `WithProjectDependencies``1` to run `carton install` at startup.
capabilities.remarks:
Carton only supports project-level dependency installation via `cpanfile`.
Calling `WithPackage``1` after `WithCarton()` will throw
because Carton does not support installing individual modules.
To install Carton, you may use "cpanm Carton".

capabilities.name: withCpanMinus
capabilities.description: Configures the Perl application to use cpanm (App::cpanminus) as its package manager instead of the default cpan. Call this before `WithPackage``1` to change how packages are installed.

capabilities.name: withLocalLib
capabilities.description: Configures the Perl application to use a local::lib directory for module isolation. Sets `PERL5LIB`, `PERL_LOCAL_LIB_ROOT`, `PERL_MM_OPT`, and `PERL_MB_OPT` environment variables so that modules are resolved from and installed into the local directory.
capabilities.remarks:
Sets the following environment variables:
-
-
-
-
These ensure modules are resolved from and installed into the local directory
without requiring system-level permissions.
If the active package manager is `cpan` (the default), it is automatically
switched to `cpanm` since `cpan` does not support the `--local-lib` flag.
While it is possible to use `cpan` with Local::Lib it is complicated to model in Aspire.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to the local::lib directory. Relative paths are resolved against the resource's working directory (`appDirectory`), not the AppHost project. Rooted paths (e.g., `/opt/perl-libs` or `C:\perl-libs`) are used as-is. Defaults to `"local"` (the Carton convention).

capabilities.name: withPackage
capabilities.description: Adds a Perl package (module) to be installed before the application starts. Uses the configured package manager: cpan by default, or cpanm if `WithCpanMinus``1` was called.
capabilities.remarks:
A child installer resource is created that runs before the main application starts.
If the module is already installed (verified via `perl -MModuleName -e 1`),
installation is skipped. The installer uses the active package manager ﻗ
`cpan` by default, or `cpanm` if `WithCpanMinus``1`
was called.
capabilities.signature: withPackage(packageName: string, force?: boolean, skipTest?: boolean): PerlAppResource

parameters.name: packageName
parameters.description: The name of the Perl module to install (e.g., "Mojolicious", "DBI").

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: If `true`, force installation even if the module is already installed or tests fail.

parameters.name: skipTest
parameters.description: If `true`, skip running tests during installation.
